perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, is, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, is, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned may be, filed on behalf of each of us at their sole discretion, notwithstanding the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be' to 'may be, filed on behalf of each of us at their sole discretion, notwithstanding the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k)' introduces a contradiction. Rule 13d-1(k) mandates the filing of amendments, whereas the modified language makes it discretionary. This introduces uncertainty in the enforcement of mandatory filing requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 requires that agreements to file jointly under Section 13(d) be filed as an exhibit to the Schedule. By stating that filing is discretionary and not in accordance with Rule 13d-1(k), the modified text directly contradicts this mandatory filing requirement.

perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the mandatory "shall be" with the discretionary "may be, filed on behalf of each of us at their sole discretion, notwithstanding the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k)". The Law Explanation states that Rule 13d-1(k) requires agreements to file jointly under Section 13(d) to be filed as an exhibit to the Schedule. By making the filing discretionary and stating it is "notwithstanding the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k)", the changed text directly contradicts the mandatory filing requirement. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions 'eCFR :: 17 CFR 240.13d-1 -- Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G', which means that the rule governs filing of these schedules. This contradiction is clear and impactful because it undermines a mandatory regulatory requirement.
